definition: An institution where one can place and borrow money and take care of financial affairs.
example: She opened a new account at the local bank.
exampleVi: Cô ấy đã mở một tài khoản mới tại ngân hàng địa phương.
collocations: open a bank account, local bank, bank branch

definition: A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.
example: Please transfer the money to my savings account.
exampleVi: Vui lòng chuyển tiền vào tài khoản tiết kiệm của tôi.
collocations: open an account, savings account, checking account

definition: The amount of money remaining in a bank account.
example: You can check your account balance using the mobile app.
exampleVi: Bạn có thể kiểm tra số dư tài khoản bằng ứng dụng di động.
collocations: account balance, check the balance, low balance

definition: A sum of money placed into a bank account.
example: He made a deposit of $500 into his savings account.
exampleVi: Anh ấy đã gửi 500 đô la vào tài khoản tiết kiệm của mình.
collocations: make a deposit, direct deposit, deposit slip

definition: To pull (something) back, aside, or away.
example: I need to withdraw some cash from my account.
exampleVi: Tôi cần rút một ít tiền mặt từ tài khoản của mình.
collocations: withdraw cash, withdraw funds, withdraw money

definition: Receiving from someone's care what one has earlier entrusted to them. Usually refers to money.
example: There is a fee for cash withdrawals at other banks' ATMs.
exampleVi: Có một khoản phí cho việc rút tiền mặt tại máy ATM của ngân hàng khác.
collocations: cash withdrawal, withdrawal fee, withdrawal limit

definition: The act of conveying or removing something from one place, person or thing to another.
example: I made a bank transfer to pay my rent this morning.
exampleVi: Tôi đã thực hiện chuyển khoản ngân hàng để trả tiền thuê nhà sáng nay.
collocations: bank transfer, wire transfer, transfer funds

definition: An act or instance of lending, an act or instance of granting something for temporary use.
example: Because of the loan that John made to me, I was able to pay my tuition for the upcoming semester.
exampleVi: Nhờ khoản vay mà John đã cho tôi vay nên tôi đã có thể trả được học phí cho học kỳ sắp tới.
collocations: apply for a loan, take out a loan, repay a loan

definition: The price paid for obtaining, or price received for providing, money or goods in a credit transaction, calculated as a fraction of the amount or value of what was borrowed.
example: Our bank offers borrowers an annual interest of 5%.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng của chúng tôi cung cấp cho người vay lãi suất hàng năm là 5%.
collocations: interest rate, earn interest, annual interest

definition: An action, state of mind, or object one has an obligation to perform for another, adopt toward another, or give to another.
example: It took him five years to pay off his student debt.
exampleVi: Anh ấy mất năm năm để trả hết khoản nợ sinh viên của mình.
collocations: pay off debt, in debt, debt collection

definition: The ability to borrow money or receive goods based on a promise of future payment.
example: She used her credit card to pay for the groceries.
exampleVi: Cô ấy dùng thẻ tín dụng để thanh toán tiền tạp hóa.
collocations: credit card, credit limit, good credit

definition: In bookkeeping, an entry in the left hand column of an account.
example: A cash sale is recorded as debit on the cash account and as credit on the sales account.
exampleVi: Việc bán hàng bằng tiền mặt được ghi nhận là ghi nợ trên tài khoản tiền mặt và ghi có trên tài khoản bán hàng.
collocations: debit card, debit an account, direct debit

definition: A small plastic card issued by a bank, used to pay for goods or withdraw money.
example: My debit card was declined at the checkout.
exampleVi: Thẻ ghi nợ của tôi bị từ chối khi thanh toán.
collocations: debit card, credit card, swipe a card

definition: a machine, usually built into an outside wall of a bank, that allows people to take out money from their account using a special card
example: I need to withdraw some cash from the ATM before we go.
exampleVi: Tôi cần rút một ít tiền mặt từ máy ATM trước khi chúng ta đi.
collocations: ATM machine, withdraw from an ATM, nearest ATM

definition: A local office of a bank or company.
example: The nearest branch is just two blocks from my office.
exampleVi: Chi nhánh gần nhất chỉ cách văn phòng của tôi hai dãy nhà.
collocations: bank branch, local branch, branch manager

definition: A person who handles cash transactions at a bank or store.
example: The cashier counted my money and handed me a receipt.
exampleVi: Nhân viên thu ngân đếm tiền của tôi và đưa cho tôi biên lai.
collocations: bank cashier, cashier's check, cashier's desk

definition: A bank employee who handles customer transactions such as deposits and withdrawals.
example: The teller helped me deposit the check into my account.
exampleVi: Nhân viên giao dịch đã giúp tôi gửi séc vào tài khoản của mình.
collocations: bank teller, teller window, ask the teller

definition: A person's name, written by that person, used as identification or to signify approval of accompanying material, such as a legal contract.
example: Please provide your signature at the bottom of the form.
exampleVi: Vui lòng ký tên ở cuối biểu mẫu.
collocations: sign a signature, signature card, verify a signature

definition: A draft directing a bank to pay money to a named person or entity.
example: I was not carrying cash, so I wrote a cheque for the amount.
exampleVi: Tôi không mang theo tiền mặt nên tôi viết séc để xác nhận số tiền đó.
collocations: write a cheque, cash a cheque, bounce a cheque

definition: A written order instructing a bank to pay money from an account (American English spelling of cheque).
example: He deposited the check at the ATM.
exampleVi: Anh ấy đã gửi tấm séc vào máy ATM.
collocations: write a check, cash a check, personal check

definition: A secure room in a bank used to store cash and valuables.
example: The bank keeps its cash reserves in a locked vault.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng cất giữ tiền mặt dự trữ trong một kho tiền có khóa.
collocations: bank vault, secure vault, store in a vault

definition: Money or other items used to facilitate transactions.
example: Wampum was used as a currency by Amerindians.
exampleVi: Wampum được người Mỹ bản địa sử dụng làm tiền tệ.
collocations: foreign currency, exchange currency, currency exchange rate

definition: Money in the form of notes/bills and coins, as opposed to cheques/checks or electronic transactions.
example: After you bounced those checks last time, they want to be paid in cash.
exampleVi: Sau khi bạn trả lại những tấm séc đó lần trước, họ muốn được thanh toán bằng tiền mặt.
collocations: pay in cash, withdraw cash, cash payment

definition: (money) A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
example: He dropped a few coins into the vending machine.
exampleVi: Anh ấy bỏ vài đồng xu vào máy bán hàng tự động.
collocations: loose coins, coin collection, coin slot

definition: A printed summary of transactions in a bank account over a period of time.
example: Check your bank statement carefully for any unauthorized charges.
exampleVi: Hãy kiểm tra kỹ sao kê ngân hàng để phát hiện các khoản phí không được ủy quyền.
collocations: bank statement, monthly statement, request a statement

definition: A charge for a service, such as one levied by a bank.
example: The bank charges a monthly maintenance fee for this account.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng thu phí duy trì hàng tháng cho tài khoản này.
collocations: maintenance fee, service fee, waive a fee

definition: The act of overdrawing a bank account.
example: He was charged a fee for going into overdraft.
exampleVi: Anh ấy bị tính phí vì bị thấu chi tài khoản.
collocations: overdraft fee, overdraft protection, go into overdraft

definition: The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.
example: The bank uses two-factor authentication to improve account security.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng sử dụng xác thực hai yếu tố để tăng cường bảo mật tài khoản.
collocations: account security, security measures, online security

definition: Money set aside for future use rather than spent.
example: She keeps her savings in a high-interest account.
exampleVi: Cô ấy giữ tiền tiết kiệm trong một tài khoản lãi suất cao.
collocations: savings account, life savings, save money

definition: The act of conducting or carrying out (business, negotiations, plans).
example: The transaction was made on Friday with the supplier.
exampleVi: Giao dịch được thực hiện vào thứ Sáu với nhà cung cấp.
collocations: financial transaction, process a transaction, transaction history

definition: The condition of failing to meet an obligation.
example: He failed to make payments on time and is now in default.
exampleVi: Anh ta đã không thanh toán đúng hạn và hiện đang bị vỡ nợ.
collocations: loan default, default on a payment, risk of default

definition: A written order for a bank to pay a specified sum of money, similar to a check.
example: The company paid the supplier using a bank draft.
exampleVi: Công ty đã thanh toán cho nhà cung cấp bằng hối phiếu ngân hàng.
collocations: bank draft, issue a draft, cash a draft

definition: A book for keeping notes, especially one for keeping accounting records; a record book, a register.
example: The bank updates its ledger after every transaction.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng cập nhật sổ cái sau mỗi giao dịch.
collocations: general ledger, update the ledger, ledger entry

definition: A fee charged by a bank or agent for carrying out a transaction or service.
example: The bank charges a small commission on foreign currency exchanges.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng thu một khoản hoa hồng nhỏ cho các giao dịch đổi ngoại tệ.
collocations: charge a commission, commission fee, earn a commission

definition: A legally declared or recognized condition of insolvency of a person or organization.
example: The company filed for bankruptcy after failing to repay its loans.
exampleVi: Công ty đã nộp đơn xin phá sản sau khi không trả được các khoản vay.
collocations: file for bankruptcy, declare bankruptcy, personal bankruptcy

definition: A special form of secured loan where the purpose of the loan must be specified to the lender, to purchase assets that must be fixed (not movable) property, such as a house or piece of farm land. The assets are registered as the legal property of the borrower but the lender can seize them and dispose of them if they are not satisfied with the manner in which the repayment of the loan is conducted by the borrower. Once the loan is fully repaid, the lender loses this right of seizure and the assets are then deemed to be unencumbered.
example: We're renting a property in the city centre because we can't afford to get a mortgage yet.
exampleVi: Chúng tôi đang thuê một căn nhà ở trung tâm thành phố vì chúng tôi chưa đủ khả năng vay thế chấp.
collocations: take out a mortgage, mortgage payment, pay off a mortgage

definition: A security or guarantee (usually an asset) pledged for the repayment of a loan if one cannot procure enough funds to repay.
example: The bank required the car as collateral for the loan.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng yêu cầu chiếc xe làm tài sản thế chấp cho khoản vay.
collocations: collateral for a loan, pledge collateral, use as collateral

definition: The amount of money levied for a service.
example: There will be a charge of five dollars.
exampleVi: Sẽ có một khoản phí năm đô la.
collocations: service charge, additional charge, free of charge

definition: The act of transmitting money, bills, etc. to a distant place, in return or payment for goods purchased.
example: He sends a monthly remittance to his family overseas.
exampleVi: Anh ấy gửi tiền hàng tháng cho gia đình ở nước ngoài.
collocations: send a remittance, remittance fee, international remittance

definition: A secure, lockable box, often rented from a bank, used to store valuables.
example: She keeps her important documents in a safebox at the bank.
exampleVi: Cô ấy giữ các giấy tờ quan trọng trong hộp ký gửi an toàn tại ngân hàng.
collocations: rent a safebox, safe deposit box, store in a safebox

definition: A person to whom a debt is owed.
example: The company must repay its creditors before declaring a profit.
exampleVi: Công ty phải trả nợ cho các chủ nợ trước khi công bố lợi nhuận.
collocations: repay a creditor, main creditor, creditors' meeting

definition: To repossess a mortgaged property whose owner has failed to make the necessary payments; used with on.
example: They have to move out of their house because the bank foreclosed on their mortgage.
exampleVi: Họ phải rời khỏi nhà vì ngân hàng tịch thu tài sản thế chấp của họ.
collocations: foreclose on a property, foreclosure notice

definition: A person or firm that owes money; one in debt; one who owes a debt
example: The bank sent a reminder letter to the debtor about the overdue payment.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng đã gửi thư nhắc nhở cho con nợ về khoản thanh toán quá hạn.
collocations: main debtor, debtor's obligation, chase a debtor

definition: A person or company that provides a guarantee.
example: My father agreed to be the guarantor for my student loan.
exampleVi: Bố tôi đồng ý làm người bảo lãnh cho khoản vay sinh viên của tôi.
collocations: act as guarantor, loan guarantor, become a guarantor

definition: Already-produced durable goods available for use as a factor of production, such as steam shovels (equipment) and office buildings (structures).
example: The bank requires businesses to have sufficient capital before approving a loan.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng yêu cầu doanh nghiệp có đủ vốn trước khi phê duyệt khoản vay.
collocations: raise capital, working capital, capital investment

definition: The degree of which something is in high supply and demand, making it easily convertible to cash
example: My Picasso painting is not very liquid, it would take me months to sell it. Gold on the other hand is convertible to cash at any moment, making it a very liquid commodity.
exampleVi: Bức tranh Picasso của tôi không có tính thanh khoản cao, tôi phải mất nhiều tháng mới bán được nó. Mặt khác, vàng có thể chuyển đổi thành tiền mặt bất cứ lúc nào, khiến nó trở thành một mặt hàng có tính thanh khoản cao.
collocations: bank liquidity, liquidity risk, maintain liquidity

definition: The state of having enough funds or liquid assets to pay all of one's debts; the state of being solvent.
example: Regulators closely monitor the solvency of major banks.
exampleVi: Các cơ quan quản lý giám sát chặt chẽ khả năng thanh toán của các ngân hàng lớn.
collocations: bank solvency, solvency ratio, maintain solvency

definition: Money kept by a bank to meet withdrawal demands or regulatory requirements.
example: The central bank requires commercial banks to hold a minimum cash reserve.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng trung ương yêu cầu các ngân hàng thương mại duy trì mức dự trữ tiền mặt tối thiểu.
collocations: cash reserve, reserve requirement, hold in reserve

definition: The government department or bank division responsible for managing funds and finances.
example: The company's treasury department manages its cash flow and investments.
exampleVi: Bộ phận kho bạc của công ty quản lý dòng tiền và các khoản đầu tư.
collocations: treasury department, treasury bond, national treasury

definition: The condition of being insolvent; the state or condition of a person who is insolvent; the condition of one who is unable to pay his debts as they fall due, or in the usual course of trade and business.
example: The company faces insolvency.
exampleVi: Công ty phải đối mặt với tình trạng phá sản.
collocations: face insolvency, insolvency proceedings, risk of insolvency

definition: One who benefits or receives an advantage.
example: You are the lucky beneficiary of this special offer.
exampleVi: Bạn là người may mắn được hưởng ưu đãi đặc biệt này.
collocations: named beneficiary, life insurance beneficiary, beneficiary account

definition: A bank employee responsible for handling customer accounts, loans, or other financial services.
example: A loan officer reviewed our application before approving the mortgage.
exampleVi: Một nhân viên tín dụng đã xem xét hồ sơ của chúng tôi trước khi phê duyệt khoản vay thế chấp.
collocations: loan officer, bank officer, compliance officer

definition: One who lends, especially money; specifically, a bank or other entity that specializes in granting loans.
example: Compare rates from several lenders before choosing a mortgage.
exampleVi: Hãy so sánh lãi suất từ nhiều bên cho vay trước khi chọn khoản vay thế chấp.
collocations: loan lender, mortgage lender, private lender

definition: One who pays; specifically, the person by whom a bill or note has been, or should be, paid.
example: The bank confirmed the payer's identity before processing the transfer.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng xác nhận danh tính người thanh toán trước khi xử lý chuyển khoản.
collocations: payer's name, verify the payer, payer account

definition: One to whom money is paid.
example: Make sure the payee's name matches the account holder.
exampleVi: Hãy đảm bảo tên người nhận khớp với chủ tài khoản.
collocations: payee's name, add a payee, payee account

definition: A lawyer of noncontentious private civil law who drafts, takes, and records legal instruments for private parties, and provides legal advice, but does not appear in court on clients' behalf.
example: The loan documents must be signed in front of a notary.
exampleVi: Các giấy tờ vay vốn phải được ký trước sự chứng kiến của công chứng viên.
collocations: notary public, visit a notary, notarize a document

definition: A person or organization that takes out a loan.
example: The bank checks a borrower's credit history before approving a loan.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng kiểm tra lịch sử tín dụng của người vay trước khi phê duyệt khoản vay.
collocations: loan borrower, first-time borrower, borrower's credit

definition: A person who makes a deposit, especially a deposit of money in a bank
example: Depositors are protected by government insurance up to a certain amount.
exampleVi: Người gửi tiền được bảo vệ bởi bảo hiểm chính phủ lên đến một hạn mức nhất định.
collocations: bank depositor, depositor protection, individual depositor

definition: Someone who transfers his property to another.
example: The transferor must confirm the transaction before the funds are moved.
exampleVi: Người chuyển nhượng phải xác nhận giao dịch trước khi tiền được chuyển đi.
collocations: transferor's account, confirm as transferor

definition: A person who signs the back of a check to transfer it to someone else.
example: The endorser signed the back of the check before depositing it.
exampleVi: Người ký hậu đã ký vào mặt sau của tấm séc trước khi gửi vào ngân hàng.
collocations: check endorser, endorser's signature

definition: A person who withdraws money from a bank account.
example: The bank recorded the withdrawer's ID number for the large cash withdrawal.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng ghi lại số CMND của người rút tiền đối với khoản rút tiền mặt lớn.
collocations: identify the withdrawer, withdrawer's account

definition: One who audits bookkeeping accounts.
example: An external auditor reviewed the bank's financial records.
exampleVi: Một kiểm toán viên độc lập đã rà soát hồ sơ tài chính của ngân hàng.
collocations: external auditor, internal auditor, bank auditor

definition: One who renders account; one accountable.
example: The bank's accountant prepared the quarterly financial report.
exampleVi: Kế toán viên của ngân hàng đã lập báo cáo tài chính quý.
collocations: chartered accountant, bank accountant, hire an accountant

definition: A person whose job is to manage something, such as a business, a restaurant, or a sports team.
example: The branch manager approved my loan application.
exampleVi: Trưởng chi nhánh đã phê duyệt đơn xin vay của tôi.
collocations: branch manager, bank manager, account manager

definition: One who signs or has signed something.
example: John Hancock is famous for being the first signatory to the American Declaration of Independence, and for writing his name large.
exampleVi: John Hancock nổi tiếng là người đầu tiên ký Tuyên ngôn Độc lập Hoa Kỳ và viết tên ông thật lớn.
collocations: authorized signatory, joint signatory, add a signatory

definition: A locked metal box used to store money or valuables securely.
example: The store manager keeps the daily cash in a safe overnight.
exampleVi: Quản lý cửa hàng cất tiền mặt hàng ngày trong két sắt qua đêm.
collocations: keep in a safe, open the safe, fireproof safe

definition: The person who sends money in a remittance transaction.
example: The remitter must provide identification before sending money abroad.
exampleVi: Người gửi tiền phải cung cấp giấy tờ tùy thân trước khi chuyển tiền ra nước ngoài.
collocations: remitter's details, verify the remitter

definition: The person who receives money in a remittance transaction.
example: The remittee received the funds within two business days.
exampleVi: Người nhận tiền chuyển đã nhận được tiền trong vòng hai ngày làm việc.
collocations: remittee's account, notify the remittee

definition: The person who receives money or assets in a transfer.
example: The transferee confirmed receipt of the funds the next day.
exampleVi: Người được chuyển nhượng đã xác nhận nhận được tiền vào ngày hôm sau.
collocations: transferee's account, notify the transferee
